feedback.text: The Digital Fairness Act is, in my opinion, a well put together piece of legislation that most definitely has EU citizens' rights in the forefront in the context of malicious practices in digital spaces. However, I would very much like to see the protections outlined within this legislation extended to video games. Specifically to deny video game publishers the ability to remove games from consumers' digital libraries after purchase, as well as to ensure said publishers keep their games playable even after their live service (i.e. content updates, patches, downloadable content, etc.) has ended. The "Stop Killing/Destroying Games" initiative outlines these ideas in full. I hope you consider implementing some of them within the policy goals of the Digital Fairness Act as, in my opinion, it would widen its reach and effectiveness. Thank you!
